name: DROID (MASTER)
description: User-programmable universal CV processor. 8 CV in, 8 CV out (16-bit, ±10V), 4×4 multicolor LED matrix. Patches are text/forge-built networks of circuits: LFOs, quantizers, sequencers, logic, MIDI — all in one module.

controls.name: Button
controls.description: Loads the patch from the SD card at droid.ini. Press after swapping the card.
controls.detail: momentary · also used for maintenance menu on long-press

controls.name: Micro SD slot
controls.description: Holds droid.ini (patch) and DROIDSTA.BIN (saved circuit states).
controls.detail: always keep card inserted during play · removing card loses state saves

controls.name: 4×4 LED matrix
controls.description: Top two rows mirror inputs I1–I8, bottom two rows mirror outputs O1–O8. Bipolar signals shown red/blue, unipolar white.
controls.detail: can be overridden with R1–R16 registers for custom display · X1 register shows a number live

controls.name: Controller bus header
controls.description: 6-pin chain to P2B8/P4B2/P10/S10/P8S8/B32/M4/E4/DB8E controllers. X7 always first if present.
controls.detail: up to 16 controllers · last controller's jumper = LAST · others = PARK

controls.name: G8 expansion header
controls.description: 8-pin chain to up to 4× G8 gate expanders.
controls.detail: adds 8–32 gate in/out jacks · red stripe down on ribbon cable

inputs.name: I1–I8
inputs.description: Eight CV inputs. Read as numbers −1.0 … +1.0 inside the patch. Every circuit input can attenuate (×) and offset (+).

outputs.name: O1–O8
outputs.description: Eight CV outputs. Patch circuit outputs to O1–O8. Any number outside −1..+1 is clamped at ±10V.

outputs.name: N1–N8 (virtual)
outputs.description: Virtual output registers that feed the normalization of I1–I8. Patch an internal LFO to N1; I1 reads that LFO when unpatched, the external signal when patched.

behaviors.name: Circuit model
behaviors.description: A patch is a list of circuit instances (lfo, quantizer, algoquencer, arpeggio, contour, mixer, logic, midiin, etc.) with each input parameter set to a fixed number, a register (I/O/P/B/L/G), a controller control, or an internal cable name. 70+ built-in circuit types cover sequencing, quantizing, envelopes, LFOs, logic, MIDI, random, slew, swing, chord generation, calibration.

behaviors.name: Main loop
behaviors.description: Entire patch is evaluated at ~5.5 kHz (≈180 µs per cycle). Circuits execute top-to-bottom in file order; internal cables resolve in one cycle if source precedes destination, else incur ~160 µs delay. Reordering circuits can shave a loop cycle of latency.

behaviors.name: Parameter columns A×B+C
behaviors.description: Every input accepts three columns: source value, multiplier/attenuation, offset. Result = A·B+C. Multiplier and offset can themselves be CVs — so every input has a built-in VCA + mixer for free.

behaviors.name: Input normalization via N registers
behaviors.description: If a circuit writes to N3, input I3 returns that signal when nothing is patched, and the external jack signal when patched. Classic use: internal clock (LFO→N1) overridden by external clock patched to I1.

behaviors.name: State saved to DROIDSTA.BIN
behaviors.description: Button toggles, sequencer steps, matrix mixer positions, calibrations — all saved to the SD card ~1.5 s after change. State survives power cycles. LFO phases, cvlooper recordings, temporary values are not saved. Per-circuit dontsave=1 disables save.

behaviors.name: Forge over X7 MIDI sysex
behaviors.description: With X7 attached, patches upload from Forge over USB-MIDI sysex in under a second. No SD card swap. The only way to iterate fast. Without X7, upload requires pulling SD card to PC, writing droid.ini, reinserting, pressing the button.

behaviors.name: Error display on LED matrix
behaviors.description: On load, errors encode the offending line number and column via coloured LEDs on the 4×4 matrix. Full error text is written to ERRORS.TXT on the SD card. X1 register sends a live number value for in-patch inspection.

behaviors.name: Internal cables
behaviors.description: Any string starting with _ is a named internal cable. One output writes it, any number of inputs read it. Equivalent to a virtual patch cable inside the DROID. Used heavily for modulation routing without eating physical jacks.

behaviors.name: Voltage ↔ number convention
behaviors.description: Input voltage is scaled: 10V ↔ 1.0, 5V ↔ 0.5, −10V ↔ −1.0. Numbers sent to O registers scale the same way out to the jack. V/oct uses 0.1 per semitone so 0.5 = 60 semitones up = C5. Numbers beyond ±1 are clipped at ±10V at the jack but used at full precision internally.

behaviors.name: One knob multiple functions
behaviors.description: Declare multiple pot circuits pointing at the same physical P1.1 with different select inputs tied to a button/buttongroup/switch output. Each pot circuit drives its own target. One pot becomes attack/decay/sustain/release on a contour — menu-style CV control. Physical position persists per virtual slot.

patchIdeas.name: Hello world · LFO to O1
patchIdeas.patch: Canonical first patch. Declare one lfo circuit · set hz=5 · route square output to O1 · load to master · O1 pulses at 5 Hz · demonstrates that a DROID patch is just a list of circuits with parameters mapped to registers.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* lfo: hz=5, square=O1

- DROID (O1) c> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Overridable internal clock
patchIdeas.patch: lfo hz=4 square=N1 · any circuit using I1 as clock reads the internal 4 Hz when I1 is unpatched · patch an external clock to I1 and the internal LFO is silently replaced · illustrates N1..N8 normalization — a patch can expose internal signals as default values on physical jacks.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* lfo: hz=4, square=N1
* contour: trigger=I1, output=O1

- External Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1) // overrides internal clock
- DROID (O1) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Quantized S&H melody
patchIdeas.patch: lfo hz=0.3 triangle=_NOISE (as pseudo-noise source) → sample input=_NOISE, trigger=I1 → minifonion input=sample.output root=P1.1 scale=P1.2 → O1 · external clock at I1 triggers a new random, quantizer snaps to scale set by two pots on a P2B8.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* random: output=_RAND
* sample: input=_RAND, trigger=I1, output=_NOTE
* minifonion: input=_NOTE, root=P1.1, scale=P1.2, output=O1

P2B8:

- External Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Euclidean drum trio
patchIdeas.patch: Three euklid circuits, one per drum · shared clock on I1 · length/fill/rotate from P1.1–P1.6 on a P10 · outputs → O1/O2/O3 → kick/snare/hat · B1.1 on a P2B8 mutes the hat via a button circuit · full rhythm box inside one 8HP module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* euklid: clock=I1, length=P1.1, fill=P1.2, output=O1
* euklid: clock=I1, length=P1.3, fill=P1.4, output=O2
* euklid: clock=I1, length=P1.5, fill=P1.6, output=O3, mute=B2.1

P10:

P2B8:

- External Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) t> Kick (Trig)
- DROID (O2) t> Snare (Trig)
- DROID (O3) t> Hat (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: One knob ADSR (pot overlay)
patchIdeas.patch: P1.1 controls four targets: attack/decay/sustain/release on a contour · buttongroup B1.1–B1.4 selects which target is active · four pot circuits overlay P1.1 with select=L1.1..L1.4 · rotating the single pot edits only the currently-lit parameter · classic DROID menu-style interaction.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* buttongroup: button1..4=B1.1..B1.4, led1..4=L1.1..L1.4
* pot: pot=P1.1, select=L1.1, output=_A
* pot: pot=P1.1, select=L1.2, output=_D
* pot: pot=P1.1, select=L1.3, output=_S
* pot: pot=P1.1, select=L1.4, output=_R
* contour: trigger=I1, attack=_A, decay=_D, sustain=_S, release=_R, output=O1

P2B8:

- Gate Source (Out) g> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Chord voice generator
patchIdeas.patch: chord circuit inputs root=I1 type=P1.1 · outputs four V/oct values → O1–O4 · four voices tuned to the chord of the current root · change root CV on I1 to transpose · turn P1.1 to morph chord type (major/minor/sus/dom7 etc.).
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* chord: root=I1, type=P1.1, output1=O1, output2=O2, output3=O3, output4=O4

P2B8:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> DROID (I1) // root
- DROID (O1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- DROID (O2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- DROID (O3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- DROID (O4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Algoquencer performance track
patchIdeas.patch: algoquencer circuit with 8 steps, buttons B1.1–B1.8 on B32 toggle activity, P1.1 controls density, P1.2 shifts rotation · clock on I1, trigger to O1, CV melody on O2 through minifonion quantized to D-minor · accent pattern on O3 via separate activity mask. A full generative track from one master+B32+P10.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* algoquencer: clock=I1, step1..8=B1.1..B1.8, trigger=O1, output=_RAW
* minifonion: input=_RAW, root=2, scale=1, output=O2

B32:

P10:

- External Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) t> Drum (Trig)
- DROID (O2) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Self-tuning VCO with calibrator
patchIdeas.patch: calibrator circuit measures VCO frequency at O1 while sweeping through known V/oct values · stores correction curve to DROIDSTA.BIN · subsequent playback through O1 is octave-perfect across six octaves · feed VCO square out back to I1 · run calibration once from a button, use daily.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* vcotuner: input=I1
* calibrator: input=I1, trigger=B1.1, output=O1

P2B8:

- VCO (Square) -> DROID (I1) // frequency measurement
- DROID (O1) p> VCO (V/Oct) // calibrated pitch

patchIdeas.name: Internal cable modulation mesh
patchIdeas.patch: lfo 0.2 Hz → _SLOW · lfo 8 Hz triangle → _FAST · sample triggered by I1 from _SLOW → _STEP · mixer mixes _FAST*0.3 + _STEP → O1 · outputs layered modulation with stepped base and audio-rate wobble, using no physical cables · the DROID as a modulation matrix.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* lfo: hz=0.2, sine=_SLOW
* lfo: hz=8, triangle=_FAST
* sample: input=_SLOW, trigger=I1, output=_STEP
* mixer: input1=_FAST, level1=0.3, input2=_STEP, output=O1

- External Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Tap tempo master clock
patchIdeas.patch: tap-tempo button on B1.1 → timing circuit outputs master clock at tapped BPM → N1 as internal clock default · euklid, algoquencer, lfo sync=clock all read I1 (falls back to the tapped tempo) · tap twice to reset · one DROID replaces a dedicated master clock module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* timing: tap=B1.1, clock=N1

P2B8:

System Clock:

- DROID (N1) c> System Clock (In) // internal default
- External Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1) // override with external
